accept_baseline_only.meaning: Accept descriptor-first research baseline and keep human syntax pressure-only.

narrow_block_syntax_pressure.meaning: Keep human syntax alive but narrow allowed grammar shape before a proposal.

reject_human_syntax_for_now.meaning: Use descriptor data only and close human syntax pressure until a later stage.

defer_for_architect_digest_policy.meaning: Defer syntax decision until source digest authority is decided.

minimum_acceptance_conditions_for_future: Must preserve CompilerProfileSpec.slot_order as canonical descriptor/future dispatch order.
Must reject inline pack implementation bodies.
Must reuse descriptor diagnostic taxonomy after lowering.
Must have pre-lowering syntax diagnostic machine codes.
Must have explicit source digest policy before golden fixtures.

grammar_questions_to_answer.question: profile slot declarations must not parse like contract declarations
implementation ids contain dots and versions; lexer/token policy must be explicit
digest policy must decide source text vs lowered AST
Which syntax errors should exist before descriptor validation runs?
grammar_questions_to_answer.research_baseline: descriptor taxonomy remains first-failure model after lowering

grammar_questions_to_answer.question: Should source order be accepted when it differs from CompilerProfileSpec.slot_order?
grammar_questions_to_answer.research_baseline: source order may vary, but lowered descriptor order must follow CompilerProfileSpec.slot_order

recommended_research_verdict.reason: Descriptor-first path is already proofable; human syntax lacks grammar and digest decisions.

non_authorizations: No grammar acceptance by Research Agent.
No profile syntax proposal is created by this packet.
No parser implementation card is opened by this packet.
